description: Canonical objection handlers keyed by the standard taxonomy. Each merges every book's objection_material for that key, maps to the canonical PRIN ids, and supplies three reply variations (warm / firm / data-backed), an escalate-or-disengage condition, and source books.

buyer_says: $45 is way too much for this
that's expensive for a used item
this brand isn't worth that much
seems high
objections.what_they_really_mean: Either a value-doubt ('convince me it's worth it'), a budget signal, or a desire to feel they got a fair deal — rarely a flat refusal.
objections.recommended_approach: Acknowledge the concern, justify with sold comps reasoning-first, hold your goal-anchor, and pivot to terms/value rather than caving on the number.

sample_replies.warm: Totally fair to push on price — I hear it feels steep for secondhand. If the price is the blocker I can cover shipping or bundle to add value. What would feel right to you?
sample_replies.firm: Yes, it's at the higher end — and that's because it's barely worn and authenticated. The way I see it, that's where the value is. I'd rather be upfront than haggle.
sample_replies.data_backed: Recent sold ones in this condition went for $48-$55 (happy to screenshot) — I'm at $50, already inside that range. I can do $47 if you close today.

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Buyer turns abusive or it converts into review-extortion -> handle as OBJ-rude-aggressive / bargaining bully.

buyer_says: is this still available?
still got this?
do you still have this in stock?
objections.what_they_really_mean: A low-signal buying cue — mild interest that needs a gentle nudge to become a real conversation.
objections.recommended_approach: Answer warmly, name yourself, and add one forward-motion step (a small-yes or one true scarcity note) instead of a dead 'yes'. Don't over-negotiate.

sample_replies.warm: Yes it is! I'm Sam — were you looking to grab it now or did you have a question about it first?
sample_replies.firm: Yes, still available. Want me to send you an offer so you can lock it in?
sample_replies.data_backed: Yes it's here! It's the last one in this size though and a few people have it saved — want me to hold it for you for a bit?

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: No escalation path; if it turns into an off-platform request, switch to OBJ-off-platform-request.

buyer_says: I'll give you $20 (listed $45)
$25? (listed $60)
what's the lowest, I'm on a budget
$20 take it or leave it
objections.what_they_really_mean: A budget cap, a habitual opening move for bragging rights, or a probe of your floor — decode the interest before reacting to the number.
objections.recommended_approach: Don't react to the number. Probe the interest/theory with a calibrated question, justify your price on comps, then either hold (strong BATNA) or make ONE labeled concession on a shrinking ladder near your list — never reflex-split.

sample_replies.warm: I appreciate the offer! What were you hoping to spend, and is this for something specific? Helps me see what I can do beyond the number.
sample_replies.firm: I appreciate it, but at $20 I'd genuinely rather hold and relist — these sell around $30-35. $28 and I ship today, otherwise no worries.
sample_replies.data_backed: I can't do $20 — recent solds are $48-$55, so I'd be way under market. How'd you get to $20? If it's budget, I might be able to bundle something.

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Buyer repeats the same lowball with hostility or insults after two exchanges -> disengage politely, leave the standing offer, relist.

buyer_says: I've seen these for $25
another seller has it cheaper
I can get this cheaper on another account
objections.what_they_really_mean: Wants reassurance they're not overpaying; may be over-estimating their alternative or bluffing — the cheaper one is often worse condition, unauthenticated, or slow.
objections.recommended_approach: Don't disparage; probe the claim ('what condition? send the link'), differentiate on the real alternative (condition, authenticity, ship speed, returns), and shape their no-deal option honestly.

sample_replies.warm: Totally fair to compare! Mine ships tracked, condition's verified in the photos, and I've 180+ 5-star sales — happy to talk it through.
sample_replies.firm: You might find a lower sticker — worth checking it's the same condition, authenticated, and ships fast. Mine is. If the cheaper one matches all that, grab it; if not, $45 here is solid.
sample_replies.data_backed: Send me the link and I'll take a real look — if it's the same item and condition I'll factor it in; the recent solds in this shape are $48-55, so they're often not actually the same.

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Buyer refuses to share the comp and keeps demanding a match below your floor -> hold, leave the standing offer.

buyer_says: what condition is it really in?
the photos are dark, I can't tell
is there any damage?
this isn't what I expected (post-sale)
objections.what_they_really_mean: A value-doubt and fear of being disappointed — needs proof and confidence, not a discount.
objections.recommended_approach: Treat as a value-doubt interest: get curious, supply proof (extra photos, measurements, grade) and contingent terms (returns), disclose real flaws honestly. Pre-sale: don't drop price. Post-sale: acknowledge feeling, own any real miss, offer options.

sample_replies.warm: Totally fair to want to be sure — I'll send extra close-ups and the measurements so you can judge before you commit. Anything specific you want a photo of?
sample_replies.firm: I disclose every flaw in the photos — there's one tiny mark on the hem, shown in pic 4. I grade it 8/10 and I accept returns if it's not as described, so there's no risk on your side.
sample_replies.data_backed: Here are close-ups of the seams and sole; last two buyers rated condition 5/5. I'd grade it 8/10 — want any other angle?

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Post-sale 'not as described' that the buyer escalates -> move to OBJ-trust-authenticity / two-option resolution; involve platform if no agreement.

buyer_says: shipping is too expensive
$8 shipping on top? no thanks
can you do free shipping?
objections.what_they_really_mean: The total feels high or there's a surprise at checkout; often cheaper to absorb shipping than to cut the item price.
objections.recommended_approach: Acknowledge ('yes, shipping adds up'), then dovetail: absorb shipping or fold it into one flat number, or use a second item to spread the one shipping fee — a low-cost-to-you, high-value-to-them trade.

sample_replies.warm: Yes, shipping does add up — what if I cover it? That effectively knocks a few dollars off for you. That's on me.
sample_replies.firm: How about I build shipping into one flat $48 so there are no surprises at checkout? Often works out better than a price drop plus postage.
sample_replies.data_backed: Grab a second item and I'll ship both for the one shipping fee — so the per-item postage basically halves.

buyer_says: any deal if I take two/three?
can you do a bundle?
would you take less on the green tee?
objections.what_they_really_mean: Wants overall value and a fair package; the highest-value integrative opportunity — they'll grow the deal if it benefits both.
objections.recommended_approach: Embrace it — move northeast: pair the wanted item with related stock at a small blended discount plus combined shipping, framed as a cooperative win and (for a stuck haggle) as a face-saving bridge. Use a labeled If/then so you never give for free.

sample_replies.warm: Love that — let's build a bundle that works for both of us. Pick three and I'll knock 15% off the total and combine shipping. Sound good?
sample_replies.firm: If you add the grey tee you liked, then I'll do both for $60 shipped — better value than either alone. Want me to set that up?
sample_replies.data_backed: Take both for $32 instead of $40 (singles) and I'll cover shipping — that's a 20% saving and one package for me.

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Buyer keeps adding items and re-cutting the total (nibble) -> name it gently, settle the whole bundle at once.

buyer_says: (silence after a question or offer)
thinking about it
maybe next payday
(committed verbally then went quiet before paying)
objections.what_they_really_mean: An unmet blocker (price, fit, trust, timing) or simple inertia; may be an avoider who flees pressure.
objections.recommended_approach: Find the blocker with a 'why not?'; revive with a single 'No'-oriented question; keep the standing offer visible with a neutral warning + true scarcity; restore freedom; then close for commitment with a written confirmation and an immediate offer/invoice.

sample_replies.warm: No rush at all! What's the one thing holding you back? If it's fit I'll send pics, if it's price tell me your ballpark. Happy to make it easy either way.
sample_replies.firm: Have you given up on this one? Totally fine if it's no longer right — just want to know whether to keep holding it for you.
sample_replies.data_backed: Just circling back — $38 with free shipping still stands. A couple people have it saved and it's the last in this size, so it may sell soon. No pressure!

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Buyer who clearly declined -> stop nudging (one nudge max), relist; a committed-then-ghosted unpaid item -> open from the Third Story once, then let the offer lapse / platform unpaid-item process.

buyer_says: let's do this on WhatsApp / Venmo F&F
I'll pay you outside the app, trust me
send it first and I'll pay after
here's a payment link / I've sent the payment (screenshot)
objections.what_they_really_mean: Either convenience-seeking or, more often, an attempt to remove buyer/seller protection so they can take the item or money (overpayment, fake-screenshot, chargeback, ship-before-pay scams).
objections.recommended_approach: SAFETY STOP — not a negotiation. Decline warmly on principle (mutual protection, not suspicion) using an 'I feel safer on the app' frame; never ship before confirmed on-platform payment; if pressed, disengage and report.

sample_replies.warm: I appreciate you wanting to make it easy! I feel more comfortable keeping it on the app though — it protects us both if anything goes sideways. Happy to go ahead at the agreed price right here whenever you're ready.
sample_replies.firm: I keep all sales on-platform for both our protection — happy to finish the deal right here, but I can't take payment or ship outside the app.
sample_replies.data_backed: On-platform payment means we're both covered by buyer/seller protection if anything goes wrong — that's why I never go off-app. I'll send the offer here and ship the moment it clears.

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Any pressure to ship before confirmed payment, an overpayment/refund ask, or a 'payment sent' screenshot you can't verify in-app -> hard stop, do not ship, report and block.

buyer_says: how do I know it's real?
is this legit? don't want to get scammed
are you a real seller?
you KNEW this was fake (post-sale accusation)
objections.what_they_really_mean: Security fear — of buying a fake or being defrauded; wants proof and a safety net. Aggression here usually springs from fear.
objections.recommended_approach: Provide genuine proof/authority (receipts, serials, detail photos, sales history, on-platform protection), decouple from personal trust, and lean on platform authentication as a contingent term. If accused post-sale, disentangle intent from impact: acknowledge the feeling first, then invite evidence.

sample_replies.warm: 100% fair to ask! I've got 180+ sales with a 4.9 rating, all items shipped tracked, and I disclose every flaw in the photos. Anything specific you'd like to see to feel comfortable?
sample_replies.firm: It's honestly not a trust thing — I keep everything through the app so we're both covered, and I stand behind every item. Here's what I can show you to verify.
sample_replies.data_backed: Photos of the date code (inside the side pocket) and the original receipt are added — hardware's engraved not stamped, a real-vs-fake tell. At $400+ it also ships through eBay's Authenticity Guarantee, so it's inspected before it reaches you.

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Post-sale fraud accusation that escalates despite evidence -> offer two-option resolution (PRIN-043), document, and let the platform's dispute process decide.

buyer_says: are you serious with this price? total ripoff
this is overpriced garbage, $10 take it or leave it
this is a joke, you're price gouging
give me 40% off or I'll leave a one-star
objections.what_they_really_mean: Often fear (of being ripped off) or a tactic to rattle you into a concession; sometimes a blunt competer. Review-extortion is a bully move, not a real objection.
objections.recommended_approach: Go to the balcony (don't react), step to their side and acknowledge, deflect the attack onto the problem with a calibrated question and a poised 'I' boundary. Don't counter-punch, don't cave. If it's a bully/extortion pattern, respond on leverage and route threats to a documented decline + report.

sample_replies.warm: Sounds like the price feels way off to you — I don't want it to feel unfair. What would feel right, and what's it based on? Happy to talk it through.
sample_replies.firm: I get that's your budget, and I can tell you're a serious buyer — and at the same time I've priced this at $28 for [reason]. I can do $25 if that helps. No pressure either way.
sample_replies.data_backed: I price fairly against recent solds ($48-55) and can't do 40% off. Reviews should reflect the actual item and service. If you'd like it at the listed price I'm glad to help — otherwise no worries. (Documenting the thread in case the platform needs it.)

objections.escalate_or_disengage_if: Persistent threats, harassment, or feedback-extortion after a fair offer -> name the dynamic once, hold the fair offer, document, report to the platform, disengage.
